title: Smoky Mediterranean Rice Pilaf
description: A fragrant rice pilaf infused with a smoky Mediterranean twist, featuring seasoned rice vinegar, binchotan charcoal aroma, and a burst of fresh herbs and vegetables, all served in a mason jar for a show-off presentation.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup rice under cold water until clear, then drain.
In a small saucepan, combine 1 cup water, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, and 1 tsp olive oil; bring to a gentle boil.
Transfer the liquid to a clean mason jar and add the rinsed rice, diced onion, minced garlic, oregano, rosemary, lemon zest, chopped tomatoes, and spinach, mixing gently.
Seal the jar tightly with the lid, then place it in a steamer or large pot with a rack; cover the pot and steam for 15 minutes at 100°C.
After steaming, remove the jar and let it sit, covered, for an additional 5 minutes so the rice continues to absorb moisture.
While the rice rests, toast pine nuts in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden, then set aside.
Open the jar, fluff the rice with a fork, and stir in crumbled low-sodium feta and toasted pine nuts.
Prepare a small bowl of binchotan charcoal and light it; once smoky, place the jar lid on the stove for 1 minute to infuse the aroma.
Remove the lid carefully, discard the charcoal, and serve the smoky Mediterranean rice pilaf immediately.
